danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat presence; enemy awareness of player
Player vulnerability state; proximity to death

danger_signals.visual_form: Gore particle effects on screen edges and environment
danger_signals.meaning: Player taking damage; active combat state

danger_signals.signal: Enemy weapon fire tracers and impacts
danger_signals.visual_form: Projectile trails, sprite flashes, environmental damage
Medieval dark stone, blood runes, unholy symbols on walls
Humanoid model with unnatural stance, glowing eyes

safety_signals.visual_form: Metallic walls, sci-fi panels, industrial design
safety_signals.meaning: Human-controlled territory; temporary refuge from demonic threat
Resource availability; support for player survival

safety_signals.visual_form: Clearly marked doors, illuminated switch plates
safety_signals.meaning: Level progression possible; mission advancement available

safety_signals.visual_form: Silent environment, no entity models visible
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary reprieve; safe to navigate/collect resources

safety_signals.visual_form: Torch flames, panel illumination, light casting
safety_signals.meaning: Navigation safety; visibility = reduced surprise threat

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Possessed soldiers (corrupted humans with glowing eyes, unnatural postures)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Futuristic military bases with sci-fi panels, corridors, technical equipment

faction_indicators.color_identity: Dark reds, blacks, sickly greens, purple hues
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Unholy symbols on walls, blood runes, demonic iconography
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Ogres (armored bestial forms), armored knights (gothic suits), lesser demons (small imp-like creatures), fiends (quadrupedal monsters)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Medieval gothic environments, demonic temples, blood-stained stone, otherworldly architecture accessed via slipgates

faction_indicators.color_identity: Brown, gray earth tones, dark ambiguous colors
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Transitional areas between military and demonic zones; slipgate chambers

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Demonic architecture visibly corrupting landscape; blood on walls; destroyed military structures showing both tech and demonic damage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Monsters actively patrol and attack; player must fight through encounters; slipgates remain open allowing reinforcement flow

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Clean metallic architecture, intact technology, organized layout with clear exits and resource caches
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Fewer monsters; safe areas for navigation; ability to prepare (collect weapons/health) before advancing

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Heavily gothic medieval style, blood-soaked stone, supernatural lighting (eerie greens, purples), unholy symbols dominant
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: High monster density; aggressive spawning behavior; resource scarcity; immediate threat pressure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Torn/warped geometry at boundaries; sci-fi portal visuals transitioning to fantasy aesthetics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Demonic forces emerging; teleportation events; unstable enemy behaviors; gateway serves as invasion focal point

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: HUD tracks rune collection; episode completion returns player to hub 'START' level visually unchanged but functionally advanced
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must sequence through four episodes; each episode restart removes prior item gains, forcing resource re-collection; final rune opens 'END' level

environmental_storytelling.example: Futuristic military base gradually transitions to medieval gothic castle within same level, showing invasion front-line and dimensional breach effects; visual clash tells story of two worlds colliding
Military base walls show blast marks and demonic blood splatter patterns indicating recent combat; older blood stains suggest previous explorer casualties
Possessed soldiers placed in military base areas show corruption of human territory; demons in gothic zones show their native environment; mixed placement shows active invasion front
Health packs and weapons cluster near exits and safe areas; dangerous demonic zones feature sparse supplies, forcing risk-taking for advancement
Teleportation devices visible in both military and demonic zones serve as literal portals for invasion story; accessing slipgates advances player deeper into enemy territory

environmental_storytelling.technique: Skill level gating and narrative progression
environmental_storytelling.example: Four difficulty paths (Easy/Medium/Hard/Nightmare) with Nightmare hidden suggest escalating invasion intensity and player testing; difficulty choice affects monster density and aggression, showing worsening situation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Fires hitscan weapons with reasonable accuracy; vocalizes guttural sounds; walks upright but moves erratically; telegraphs attacks before firing
Fires chainsaw bursts in spread pattern; charges toward player; makes loud roaring vocalizations; heavily armored visually (chainmail, helmets)
Sword and shield wielder; closes melee range; slower movement suggests armor weight; high health indicator through visual bulk
Quadrupedal locomotion; fast aggressive sprinting toward player; leap attacks with grab potential; unnatural joint angles suggest demonic nature
Fast erratic movement; small sprite size suggests lesser threat individually; spawn in groups for cumulative danger; high-pitched vocalizations
Weapon models visible in lower right screen-space; damage feedback shows blood screen edge effects; movement restricted by terrain and physics; weapon firing creates screen recoil animation

notes: Quake (1996) uses strong visual dichotomy between futuristic military aesthetic and gothic-demonic environments to communicate world-state of dimensional invasion. Danger is signaled through monster proximity, visual corruption of space (blood/runes), and architecture style shifts. Safety manifests through military base design, item glows, and clean technical environments. Faction identity relies heavily on color grading (cold metallics vs. warm demonic reds/purples), environmental architecture (sci-fi vs. gothic), and enemy visual design (corrupted humans vs. supernatural beasts). The slipgate mechanic serves dual purpose: narrative invasion vector and visual world-state indicator. Episode structure with rune collection creates persistent progression signal. No explicit political factions exist; conflict is existential (human dimension vs. invading demonic force).
